text.title: rag-eval-ab-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rag-eval-ab-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running all A questions first, then all B questions, without interleaving.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM API performance, index state, and model weight updates can differ between batch runs. Non-interleaved A/B tests confound configuration differences with temporal effects, producing unreliable comparisons.

failure-mode.description: Treating compare_results() latency output as a sufficient signal for configuration decisions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The baseline RAGABTest only measures latency and source count. Quality metrics (faithfulness, precision@5, MRR) are not computed. A faster config that returns less relevant sources looks better in compare_results but is worse for users.
